历年位次与招生计划.title: Historical Rankings and Enrollment Plan
历年位次与招生计划.text: From 2021 to 2025, the minimum ranking for Physics Category was approximately 8714 → 7974 → 9701 → 9421 → 13439, with a significant backward shift in 2025 (about 4000 places compared to 2024), showing a fluctuating backward trend over the past 5 years; the 2025 baseline is about 13439; the provincial Physics Category enrollment plan was 21 in 2021, 23 in 2022, and 22 from 2023 to 2025; the 2026 plan increases to 30, an expansion of about 36.36% compared to 2025, significantly increasing admission opportunities, which is the main driving factor for the ranking backward shift.

学校层次与声誉.title: University Level and Reputation
学校层次与声誉.text: 211 Project, Double First-Class university; ranked approximately 82nd in the Soft Science Chinese University Rankings (2026), stable in the 82–84 range in recent years, at the upper-middle level nationally; reputation was slightly positive in 2025 (Journalism and Communication rated A++), but turned slightly negative in 2026 (dormitory fire incident); Douyin popularity ranked 11th (high heat), with high short video exposure and considerable application attention; Journalism and Communication is an A+ discipline (top nationally), with majors like Communication Studies and Advertising (Computational Advertising Dual Bachelor's Experimental Class) directly benefiting; Computer and Electronic Information majors, though not A-rated, have clear employment directions due to the media industry characteristics.

专业热度与地域.title: Major Popularity and Region
专业热度与地域.text: Electronic Information/Communication majors are highly recommended socially (2024), Computer majors recommended (67.8), Electrical Engineering highly recommended (74.28), overall engineering majors have high popularity; the province's 2026 Physics Category examinees are about 206,500, an increase of about 19.7% from 172,468 in 2025, significantly intensifying competition.

预测结论.title: Prediction Conclusion
预测结论.text: Considering the 2025 baseline ranking of 13439, the major benefit of a 36% expansion in the 2026 Physics Category enrollment plan, the intensified competition from a 20% increase in provincial Physics Category examinees, and the offsetting effect of high engineering major popularity, the 2026 minimum ranking is expected to continue shifting backward (easier admission), but the shift magnitude is constrained by the surge in examinees; predicted minimum ranking is about 16421, with a range of 15921–16921.
